print-comments-and-errors.label: Print, Comments, and First Errors
print-comments-and-errors.summary: Use print statements and comments while learning how syntax errors appear.

quiz_1.title: What does the print function do?
quiz_1.hint: Think about what you see when you run a program.

help.concept: The print function outputs text or variables to the console.
help.hint_1: It is commonly used for debugging and displaying information.
help.hint_2: Look for the option that describes showing information on the screen.

options.a: It displays output on the screen.
options.b: It saves data to a file.

quiz_2.title: What will happen if you forget a closing parenthesis in a print statement?
quiz_2.hint: Consider what happens when the code cannot be understood.

help.concept: A syntax error indicates that the code is not written correctly.
help.hint_1: Python will not execute the code until the error is fixed.
help.hint_2: Think about the term used for mistakes in code structure.

options.a: The program will run without any issues.
options.d: The program will print an error message.

quiz_3.title: Which of the following are valid comments in Python?
quiz_3.hint: Look for the syntax used for comments in Python.

help.concept: Comments in Python start with a # symbol.
help.hint_1: Only one option uses the correct syntax for comments.
help.hint_2: Remember that comments are ignored by the interpreter.

quiz_4.title: Complete the print statement: print('Hello, ____!')
quiz_4.hint: Focus on the missing concept or keyword.

help.concept: This task checks the key idea behind complete the print statement: print('hello, ____!').
help.hint_1: Look for the word or phrase that best matches the lesson concept.
help.hint_2: Compare the blank with the meaning of each choice, not just the wording.

quiz_5.title: Fix the Syntax Error
quiz_5.prompt: The following code has a syntax error. Fix it so that it runs correctly.
quiz_5.hint: Break the task into the main code steps first.

help.concept: This task checks whether you can apply fix the syntax error in a working solution.
help.hint_1: Identify the inputs, required steps, and expected result before writing the full solution.
help.hint_2: Build the solution one piece at a time, then check that its behavior matches the prompt.

sketch_1.title: Understanding Print Statements
sketch_1.bodyMarkdown:
In Python, the `print()` function is used to display output. For example, `print('Hello, World!')` will show `Hello, World!` on the screen. This is a great way to see what your program is doing. 

**Example:**
```python
print('This is my first Python program!')
```

**Your turn:** Try printing your name using the `print()` function!

sketch_2.title: Using Comments
sketch_2.bodyMarkdown:
Comments are used to explain code and are ignored by the Python interpreter. You can create a comment by starting a line with `#`. For example:

```python
# This is a comment
print('Hello!')  # This prints a greeting
```

**Try this:** Add a comment to your previous print statement explaining what it does.

Line by line: read each statement from top to bottom. Notice which names are created, which values are passed into functions, and what output the code is meant to show.

sketch_3.title: Identifying Syntax Errors
sketch_3.bodyMarkdown:
Syntax errors occur when Python cannot understand your code. For example, forgetting a closing parenthesis will cause an error:

```python
print('Hello, World!'
```

When you run this code, Python will show an error message. **Example:** Fix the error in the above code by adding the missing parenthesis. 

**Try it yourself:** Write a line of code that has a syntax error and see what happens!
